pi_items.source: Confirmed directory /home/ga/snap_projects/ exists and is writable by user ga during live testing. setup_task.sh creates and cleans this directory. Path matches task description.
Confirmed directory /home/ga/snap_exports/ exists and is writable by user ga during live testing. setup_task.sh creates and cleans this directory. Path matches task description.

File exists, 10,133,205 bytes. Downloaded from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/opengeos/data/main/raster/landsat.tif. Confirmed during live testing: valid GeoTIFF, 4 bands (B1, B2, B3, B4), uint8, 2127x1564 pixels, WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator (EPSG:3857). Source dataset is LANDSAT_LE7_TOA_5YEAR (Landsat 7 TOA reflectance, 1999-2003).

File exists, 6,004,315 bytes. Downloaded from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/opengeos/data/main/raster/dem.tif. Confirmed during live testing: valid GeoTIFF, little-endian byte order, 1 band named 'elevation' in SNAP, float data type. Geographic location is Oregon (~44N latitude).

The file is from LANDSAT_LE7_TOA_5YEAR dataset. Landsat 7 ETM+ standard band assignments are: B1=Blue (0.45-0.52um), B2=Green (0.52-0.60um), B3=Red (0.63-0.69um), B4=NIR (0.77-0.90um). This contradicts the task description claim that B1=SWIR1 and B2=NIR. Confirmed by WebFetch of the source file metadata and cross-referenced with the terrain_vegetation_composite task's validated_pi.json which independently documented the same contradiction. However, this same incorrect mapping is used consistently across ALL snap_env tasks (compute_ndvi, tasseled_cap_transformation, etc.), making it the de facto convention for the benchmark.

pi_items.metadata_value: not in metadata, but described in vlm_checklist privileged_info
pi_items.verified_value: B1_M, B2_M, B3_M, B4_M, elevation_S, collocationFlags
pi_items.source: Confirmed by running SNAP GPT Collocation operator on the actual data files during live testing. The Landsat master bands receive _M suffix, the DEM slave band becomes elevation_S (not band_1_S). An additional collocationFlags band (int8) is automatically created by SNAP.

pi_items.metadata_value: NDVI from NIR and Red bands: (B2_M - B3_M) / (B2_M + B3_M)
pi_items.verified_value: Expression (B2_M - B3_M) / (B2_M + B3_M) is syntactically valid and produces float32 values from -0.204 to 0.429. However, since B2 is actually Green (not NIR) and B3 is Red, this computes GRVI (Green-Red Vegetation Index), not true NDVI. True NDVI would require (B4_M - B3_M) / (B4_M + B3_M).
pi_items.source: Ran GPT BandMaths on the collocation output during live testing. 3,304,640 valid pixels out of 3,326,628 total (21,988 NaN from 0/0 in nodata areas). NDVI>0.3: 9,266 pixels (0.3%). NDVI 0.15-0.3: 141,139 pixels (4.2%). NDVI<=0.15: 3,154,235 pixels (94.8%). This follows the same convention as all other snap_env tasks.

pi_items.metadata_value: not in metadata, but relevant for task validity
pi_items.verified_value: Landsat (California, ~36-38N) and DEM (Oregon, ~44N) do NOT geographically overlap
pi_items.source: Confirmed by inspecting elevation_S values after collocation during live testing. All elevation_S values are denormalized near-zero (~3.143e-319 in float64), indicating no valid elevation data in the Landsat footprint. The collocationFlags band is all zeros, confirming no spatial overlap. The classification expression therefore degenerates to purely NDVI-based: class 3 for ~0.3% of pixels, class 2 for ~4.2%, class 1 for ~95.5%.

pi_items.verified_value: Expression syntax is valid in SNAP's BandMaths expression parser. The && and ternary ? : operators are supported. References to NDVI (virtual band) and elevation_S (collocation band) are resolved correctly when NDVI is created first as a virtual band on the same product.
pi_items.source: Verified by running GPT BandMaths with the expression (using inlined NDVI) on the collocation output. Expression parsed and executed successfully. In the GUI workflow, the agent creates NDVI first as a virtual band, then the classification expression can reference it by name.

pi_items.verified_value: Feasible. The Landsat image is 2127x1564 pixels, so a 200x200 subset from pixel 100-299 in both X and Y is well within bounds. SNAP Subset operator is available (confirmed via GPT -h).
pi_items.source: Image dimensions confirmed during live testing via PIL (2127x1564). SNAP Subset operator confirmed available via GPT operator listing.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses two real datasets from github.com/opengeos/data. The Landsat file (landsat_multispectral.tif) is a 4-band Landsat 7 TOA image (2127x1564 px, uint8, EPSG:3857) with bands named B1-B4 in SNAP. The DEM file (srtm_dem.tif) has a single band named 'elevation'. After collocation with Landsat as master, bands become B1_M, B2_M, B3_M, B4_M (Landsat), elevation_S (DEM), and collocationFlags (auto-generated). CRITICAL: The Landsat and DEM do NOT geographically overlap (California vs Oregon), so elevation_S values are all near-zero after collocation. The classification will degenerate to purely NDVI-based, but the agent should still write the expression referencing both NDVI and elevation_S. The NDVI expression (B2_M - B3_M) / (B2_M + B3_M) produces values from -0.204 to 0.429 with ~94.8% of pixels having NDVI <= 0.15, ~4.2% between 0.15-0.3, and only ~0.3% above 0.3. ALSO CRITICAL: The task description claims B1=SWIR1, B2=NIR, B3=Red, B4=Green, but the actual Landsat 7 bands are B1=Blue, B2=Green, B3=Red, B4=NIR. This same incorrect mapping is used across all snap_env tasks. Expected output: BEAM-DIMAP at ~/snap_projects/suitability_assessment.dim and GeoTIFF subset at ~/snap_exports/suitability_subset.tif (200x200 pixels from coordinates 100-299).
